content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments are valid. Amendment fr-15-270499dd identifies a real consistency bug where prompt_review.rs lacks the in-loop early-exit quorum check present in completion.rs and final_review.rs, causing wasted invocations when quorum is impossible. Amendment fr-15-d2053cf4 identifies that HTTP 402/403 quota responses from OpenRouter are not classified as BackendExhausted, leading to futile retries instead of graceful degradation.

## Votes

- `fr-15-270499dd`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading the code. prompt_review.rs:210-231 handles BackendExhausted by incrementing total_exhausted_count and doing `continue;` with no early-exit quorum check. In contrast, completion.rs:192-206 computes effective_min and checks `executed_voters.len() + completers.len().saturating_sub(i + 1) < effective_min`, breaking early if quorum is impossible. final_review.rs has the same pattern at lines 334-347 (proposal phase) and 926-934 (vote phase). The post-loop quorum check at prompt_review.rs:285-288 does exist but only fires after all remaining validators have been invoked unnecessarily. This is a genuine consistency defect — all three panel execution loops should have the same early-exit pattern to avoid wasting time and tokens invoking validators when quorum is already mathematically impossible.
- `fr-15-d2053cf4`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading openrouter_backend.rs:343-379. The map_http_error function handles 401/403 as BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None, and only runs is_backend_exhausted on 429 responses (line 354). HTTP 402 falls through to the catch-all at line 373-377 as TransportFailure. Similarly, send_probe (lines 210-216) only checks exhaustion patterns for status 429. The is_backend_exhausted function in process_backend.rs has comprehensive pattern matching for quota/billing messages ("quota exceeded", "credits exhausted", "insufficient_quota", "billing hard limit", etc.), but these patterns are never checked against 402/403 response bodies in the OpenRouter adapter. OpenRouter and upstream providers can return 402 Payment Required or 403 Forbidden with quota exhaustion messages, which would be retried as transient failures instead of triggering graceful degradation. The fix should apply the is_backend_exhausted body check on at least 402/403 responses in both send_probe and map_http_error.
